title: Drive unit
abstract: A piezoelectric drive unit is configured for driving a passive element (4) relative to an active element (1), wherein the active element (1) comprises a resonator (2) with two arms (21, 22) each extending in parallel to a reference plane (28) and ending in a contact element (31, 32) which is movable by oscillating movements of the arms (21, 22) and thereby drives the passive element (4). Each of the arms (21, 22) comprises, at the outer end of the arm, a protrusion extending inward, toward the other arm, and on at least one of the arms (21, 22), the respective contact element (31, 32), arranged at the arm's outer end, extends outward, away from the other arm.
claims:
Claims P A T E N T C L A I M S 1. A drive unit for driving a passive element (4) relative to an active element (1), wherein the active element (1) comprises a resonator (2) and at least one excitation means (23) for exciting oscillations in the resonator (2), the resonator (2) comprising at least two arms (21, 22) extending from a connection region (20) of the resonator (2) at the same side of the connection region (20), the resonator (2) and the arms (21, 22) extending in parallel to a reference plane (28), each of the arms (21 , 22) comprising, at an outer end of the arm, a contact element (31, 32), the contact elements (31, 32) being movable by way of oscillating movements of the arms (21, 22), the passive element (4) being arranged to be driven and moved relative to the active element (1) by way of these oscillating movements; the passive element (4) comprises contact areas (41, 42), each contact area (41, 42) being arranged to be in contact with a respective one of the contact elements (31, 32); characterised in that each of the arms (21, 22) comprises, at the outer end of the arm, a protrusion (33, 34) extending in a first direction, the first direction being inward, toward the other arm or outward, away from the other arm, and on at least one of the arms (21, 22), the respective contact element (31, 32) arranged at the armâs outer end extends in a second direction, the second direction being opposite to the first direction. 2. The drive unit of claim 1, wherein on both of the arms (21, 22), the respective contact element (31, 32) arranged at the armâs outer end, extends in the second direction. 

3. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein, for each of the arms (21, 22), the stiffness of the arm with respect to rotation around an axis normal to the reference plane, has a minimum value at a location of the arm that is inward of an end section of the arm comprising the respective protrusion (33, 34) and optionally also the respective contact element (31, 32). 4. The drive unit of claim 3, wherein the resonator (2) is shaped from a flat piece of material, and an end section of the arm comprising the protrusions (33, 34) and optionally the contact elements (31, 32) is separated from the remainder of the arm by a necking (35). 5. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein the first arm (21) comprises, in a section opposite to the outer end, a first further contact element (38) extending outward, away from the second arm (22), and the second arm (22) comprises, in a section opposite to the outer end, a second further contact element (39). extending outward, away from the first arm (21). 6. A drive unit for driving a passive element (4) relative to an active element (1), wherein the active element (1) comprises a resonator (2) and at least one excitation means (23) for exciting oscillations in the resonator (2), the resonator (2) comprising a first pair of arms (21, 22) extending from a connection region (20) of the resonator (2) at the same side of the connection region (20), the resonator (2) and the arms (21 , 22) extending in parallel to a reference plane (28), each of the arms (21 , 22) comprising, at an outer end of the arm, a contact element (31, 32), 

 the contact elements (31, 32) being movable by way of oscillating movements of the arms (21, 22), the passive element (4) being arranged to be driven and moved relative to the active element (1) by way of these oscillating movements; the passive element (4) comprises contact areas (41, 42), each contact area (41, 42) being arranged to be in contact with a respective one of the contact elements (31, 32); characterised in that the resonator (2) comprises a second pair of arms (21a, 22a) extending from the connection region (20) of the resonator (2) in the opposite direction as the first pair of arms (21, 22). 7. The drive unit of claim 6, wherein each of the arms of the first pair of arms (21 , 22) comprises, at the outer end of the arm, a protrusion (33, 34) extending inward, toward the other arm, and on at least one of the arms of the first pair of arms (21, 22), the respective contact element (31, 32) arranged at the armâs outer end extends outward, away from the other arm. 8. The drive unit of claim 6 or 7, wherein each of the arms of the second pair of arms (21a, 22a) comprises, at the outer end of the arm, a protrusion (33, 34) extending inward, toward the other arm, and on at least one of the arms of the second pair of arms (21a, 22a), the respective contact element (31, 32) arranged at the armâs outer end extends outward, away from the other arm. 9. The drive unit of claim 6 or 7 or 8, wherein each of the arms of the second pair of arms (21a, 22a) comprises, at the outer end of the arm, a protrusion (33, 34) extending inward, toward the other arm, and 

 on at least one of the arms of the second pair of arms (21a, 22a), the respective contact element (31a, 32a) arranged at the armâs outer end is part of the respective protrusion extending inward. 10. The drive unit of one of claims 6 to 9, wherein the shape of first and second pair of arms are substantially mirror-images of one another. 11. A drive unit for driving a passive element (4) relative to an active element (1), wherein the active element (1) comprises a resonator (2) and at least one excitation means (23) for exciting oscillations in the resonator (2), the resonator (2) comprising at least two arms (21, 22) extending from a connection region (20) of the resonator (2) at the same side of the connection region (20), the resonator (2) and the arms (21, 22) extending in parallel to a reference plane (28), each of the arms (21, 22) comprising, at an outer end of the arm, a contact element (31, 32), the contact elements (31, 32) facing outward, away from the other arm, the contact elements (31, 32) being movable by way of oscillating movements of the arms (21, 22), the passive element (4) being arranged to be driven and moved relative to the active element (1) by way of these oscillating movements; the passive element (4) comprises contact areas (41, 42), each contact area (41, 42) being arranged to be in contact with a respective one of the contact elements (31, 32); wherein the resonator (2) is shaped from a flat piece of material, characterised in that between each of the arms (21, 22) and the connection region (20) there is an attachment necking (36). 

12. The drive unit of claim 11, wherein each of the arms (21, 22), from the attachment necking (36) comprises an active section (21c, 22c) that extends in a first direction parallel to the resonator axis (24) towards the respective outer end with the respective contact element (31, 32), and a counterweight section (2 Id, 22d), that extends in a second direction opposite to the first direction. 13. The drive unit of one of claims 11 to 12, wherein for each of the arms (21, 22), the stiffness of the arm, with respect to rotation around an axis normal to the reference plane, in the counterweight section (2 Id, 22d) and in the active section (21c, 22c) where it is joined to the counterweight section (2 Id, 22d) is smaller than in the attachment necking (36). 14. The drive unit of claim 13, wherein the stiffness in the active section (21c, 22c) up to and not including a necking at which the arm is bent outward toward the respective contact element (31,32) is smaller than in the attachment necking (36). 15. The drive unit of one of claims 11 to 14, wherein the excitation means (23) covers at least the attachment necking (36) and part of the counterweight section (2 Id, 22d) and part of the active section (21c, 22c). 16. The drive unit of claim 15, wherein the excitation means (23) covers cut-out regions in the resonator (2) that create the attachment necking (36). 17. A drive unit for driving a passive element (4) relative to an active element (1), wherein the active element (1) comprises a resonator (2) and at least one excitation means (23) for exciting oscillations in the resonator (2), the resonator (2) comprising at least two arms (21, 22) extending from a connection region (20) of the resonator (2) at the same side of the connection region (20), 

 the resonator (2) and the arms (21, 22) extending in parallel to a reference plane (28), each of the arms (21, 22) comprising, at an outer end of the arm, a contact element (31, 32), the contact elements (31, 32) being movable by way of oscillating movements of the arms (21, 22), the passive element (4) being arranged to be driven and moved relative to the active element (1) by way of these oscillating movements; the passive element (4) comprises contact areas (41, 42), each contact area (41, 42) being arranged to be in contact with a respective one of the contact elements (31, 32); wherein the resonator (2) is shaped from a flat piece of material, characterised in that in regions covered by the excitation means (23), the resonator (2) comprises holes or openings (37). 18. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein the resonator (2) comprises a first surface and an opposed second surface, both parallel to the reference plane (28), and wherein one excitation means (23) is arranged on the first surface and optionally wherein another excitation means (23) is arranged on the second surface. 19. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein the passive element (4) is arranged to translate along a linear movement axis (26), the linear movement axis (26) being parallel to the reference plane (28), and in particular also parallel to the resonator axis (24). 20. The drive unit of one claims 1 to 18, wherein the passive element (4) is arranged to rotate around a rotary movement axis (29), the rotary movement axis (29) being 

 parallel to the reference plane (28), and in particular also perpendicular to the resonator axis (24). 21. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein a wear suppressing element is arranged on the passive element (4) in the contact areas (41, 42, 41a, 42a). 22. The drive unit of claim 21 , wherein the wear suppressing part is made of a material with a higher degree of hardness than a surrounding region of the passive element (4) or is created by a hardening treatment of the material of the passive element. 23. The drive unit of one of claims 21 to 22, wherein the wear suppressing part is made of a ceramic material. 24. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein the contact elements (31, 32) each comprise flat region. 25. The drive unit of claim 24, wherein a resonator length is defined as the dimension of the resonator along the resonator axis (24), from the ends of the arms (21 , 22) to the opposing ends of their counterweight sections, and wherein the extension (d) of each flat region, projected onto the reference plane (28), is between one tenth and one hundredth of the resonator length, in particular between one twentieth and one eightieth of the resonator length. 26. The drive unit of claim 25, wherein the length of the resonator (2) is between three and five millimetres, in particular four millimetres, and the extension (d) of the flat region is between 0.05 millimetres and 0.15 millimetres, in particular between 0.08 millimetres and 0.12 millimetres, in particular 0.1 millimetres. 

27. The drive unit of one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface of the resonator (2) and/or the passive element (4) is treated with high precision vibratory finishing or chemical polishing.
